title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Language Clinic: Data Minimization
summary: A release analyzes arbitrary paths without limits, logs text excerpts, follows swapped symlinks, treats a digest as authenticity, cannot reproduce its wheel, and has no rollback or incident drill. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the data minimization increment in production Bookbot release and residual-risk…
objectives: Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_security_recovery_defense_3(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Build Data Minimization evidence · Theory Data Minimization
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-data-minimization-1wgxyr
# Competency: bookbot-data-minimization.
# Case 7301: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_data_minimization_1wgxyr(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wgxyr = "1wgxyr-ryxgw1-7301"
    assert evidence_variant_1wgxyr
    evidence_axes_1wgxyr = (
        "corpus1wgxyr",
        "policyryxgw1",
        "revision7301",
        "command1wgxyr7301",
        "reportryxgw17301",
        "test73011wgxyr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wgxyr)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Data Minimization model · Theory Data Minimization, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Data Minimization precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Data Minimization model · Theory Data Minimization, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 7866 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
